pi_items.source: Verified via task instructions and external search for the real Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA).

Confirmed via historical records of the Event Horizon Telescope's first major observing campaign of Sgr A* and M87* in April 2017.

Verified task targets. Astropy calculation independently confirmed Sgr A* is well above the horizon (74.69 deg altitude) at this time and location. Alnasl (Gamma Sagittarii) is confirmed as a nearby bright star proxy.
Task strictly instructs to disable atmosphere, disable landscape, and enable azimuthal grid, and verifier.py asserts these exactly.
Verified by checking export_result.sh and verifier.py expectations.
Verified by checking export_result.sh and setup_task.sh expectations.
Task explicitly asks to take a Stellarium screenshot.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real Stellarium astronomical software to simulate the historic April 10, 2017 Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) observations from the ALMA Observatory. ALMA is verifiably situated in the Atacama Desert at -23.019° lat (-0.40175 rad) and -67.753° lon (-1.18252 rad) with an elevation of 5058 meters. The agent must aim for Sagittarius A* (or its proxy, the nearby star Alnasl) which is independently calculated to be well above the horizon (74.69° altitude) at the specified time of 08:00 UTC. The agent must accurately reflect a radio astronomy observation by configuring specific Stellarium display settings (atmosphere off, landscape off, azimuthal grid on), take a screenshot, and generate a scheduling report on the Desktop.
